Title: Wheat with greatly reduced accumulation of free asparagine in the grain, produced by CRISPR/Cas9 editing of asparagine synthetase gene TaASN2

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of acrylamide formed during baking, toasting and other high-temperature processing of wheat-based foods; reduction improves food safety.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of TaASN2 greatly reduces free asparagine accumulation in grain; triple-null edited lines showed up to >90% reduction in T2 seeds and about 48–57% of wildtype levels in T3 grain flour, while an A-genome-null line showed a smaller reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 editing of all six TaASN2 alleles in hexaploid wheat produced triple-null lines with drastic reductions in grain free asparagine. 2. The strongest line (59) showed mean free asparagine of 0.14 mmol/kg in T2 seeds, about 9% of wildtype, and remained substantially lower than wildtype in T3 grain. 3. Partial knockout limited to the A-genome homeolog also reduced grain free asparagine, consistent with relatively high expression of the A-genome TaASN2 copy. 4. Reduced asparagine was accompanied by increased free glutamine, glutamate and aspartate, indicating altered amino acid partitioning. 5. Very low-asparagine seeds showed poor germination, reversible by exogenous asparagine.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Asparagine biosynthesis / nitrogen amino acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ATP-dependent asparagine synthesis from aspartate using glutamine as amide donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent transfer of an amino group from glutamine to aspartate to form asparagine, with glutamate as co-product.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for grain free asparagine accumulation in wheat seed, specifically via the seed-expressed TaASN2 isoform.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Asparagine synthetase; N-terminal glutamine amidotransferase domain and synthetase domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seed-specific; TaASN2 is described as the only wheat asparagine synthetase gene expressed seed-specifically. The A-genome TaASN2 copy is relatively highly expressed.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Grain/seed-specific expression inferred in wheat grain; A-genome homeolog relatively highly expressed.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: grain; seed; wholemeal flour from grain
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-temperature food processing relevance for acrylamide formation; probable heat stress during grain development in the glasshouse increased free asparagine in T3 seeds; exogenous asparagine treatment rescued poor germination.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Poor germination in low-asparagine seeds; altered amino acid balance with increased glutamine, glutamate and aspartate; field performance not yet tested. Some lines showed increased fifty grain weight, but not the lowest-asparagine line 59.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of multiple homeologs; amplicon NGS genotyping across A/B/D genomes; grain free amino acid profiling by GC-MS and HPLC; segregation analysis across T1-T3 generations; phenotypic assessment; exogenous asparagine rescue of germination.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: EnsemblPlants references provided in Data S1; specific IDs not included in supplied text
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon next-generation sequencing (Illumina MiSeq) of edited TaASN2 target regions; metabolite profiling of free amino acids by GC-MS and HPLC. No public accession number reported in supplied text.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter driving wheat codon-optimized Cas9 (transformation construct context)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for developing low-acrylamide wheat varieties by genome editing or allele selection. Editing all three homeologs is most effective; A-genome targeting alone gives partial reduction. Deployment should consider possible germination issues and environmental stress effects, and selection of Cas9-free edited lines is feasible.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study distinguished TaASN2 from background paralogs TaASN1, TaASN3.1, TaASN3.2 and TaASN4, which were intentionally not targeted. Editing often continued into later generations, causing allele segregation. Some low-asparagine plants were Cas9-free by T2. The rise in T3 free asparagine under probable heat stress suggests other ASN genes can contribute under stress, but TaASN2 remains the major determinant for grain accumulation.
